name: Chicken Clemenceau
recipeInstructions:
1. Coat the chicken lightly with flour.
2. Brown in butter and oil at medium-high heat.
3. Lower the heat, add head of garlic, and saute until aromatic.
4. Pour off fat except 2 tablespoons, then add 1/2 cup each of water and wine.
5. Simmer over low heat for 1-1/2 hours, adding water and wine as needed.
6. About 15 minutes before serving, heat 1/2 cup of oil in a skillet and pan fry potatoes and additional garlic until nearly done.
7. Add peas and heat through.
8. Remove vegetables with a slotted spoon.
9. Add to chicken just before serving and heat briefly to blend flavors.
10. There should be enough sauce, of a glaze-like consistency, to coat the chicken and vegetables lightly.
